TRANSCRIPT / Bonus / Book Launch / Bestseller Campaigns
TRANSCRIPT
Now I want to talk about bestseller campaigns. If you want to have a bestseller, the
thing to remember is…hitting the bestseller list comes down to focusing your
book sales into a specific period of time. The way the New York Times list works is
that that list comes out every week and it's based on sales in the prior week. For
Amazon, it's a real time thing, it’s how many sales are happening at that exact
moment. Really, you want to focus your sales within a day, or whatever.
As you've seen with all of Product Launch Formula® is you can take the formula, and
you can mold it to give you the kind of outcome you want, but if you want to hit the
bestseller list, then you have to mold it to give you the outcome, the results in terms
of the bestseller list, in terms of putting you on that bestseller list. You basically have
to play by the rules of whichever list you're going for.
I imagine that most of the people that are going to be watching this, most of my PFL
owners, you're not going to have the massive leverage and the massive platform that
I've got, so you might be going more for an Amazon campaign. That's a lot simpler,
but I will walk you through the New York Times as well.
The rules on this are murky especially in terms of Amazon. It seems like with Amazon,
it's mostly about sales concentrated in a very short period of time within over a period
of hours, as best as we can tell. The New York Times, it's a little more murky. The rules
in New York Times is a popularity contest. It's clear it's not just purely sales…because
they cut me off the list in a week, where I know I sold more than the books that were
on the list, and they didn't put me on the list.
The second week, they did. Why? I don't know. I don't know. I have some guesses, but
you probably don't want to hear my guesses…it wouldn't be right to sit here and
make guesses and have you possibly take that as gospel, but the rules are murky and
they do change constantly, even in terms of not too long ago, the New York Times
wouldn't count ebook sales, like Kindle sales. Now they count it.
Then they keep on changing stuff like that all the time, whether they count Amazon or
not, who knows? Also, they have different categories of what category they put you in.
Sometimes it's really clear, but sometimes it's not clear. So there’s a caveat here. The
rules, they do keep changing, and they aren't 100% clear.
For Amazon, it's really about focusing within a single day or even less. When we
made our run for the top of Amazon, we really tried to focus it within a single
morning. If you're going to go for this, when you get the bestseller, take a screen shot,
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 7 --

TRANSCRIPT / Bonus / Book Launch / Bestseller Campaigns
because you don't get a medal from Amazon. They don't send you a blue ribbon or
anything. If you don't see it and grab the screen shot, then it never happened, so be
sure to get a screen shot.
Also, to be a bestseller on Amazon…I was going for the overall best book, number
one book. I didn't make it, so I think it hit number four. It might've been number three,
but I'm pretty sure it was number four on the list. There's also all kinds of
subcategories. For someone to call themselves a bestseller, they just have to hit
the bestseller list in any subcategory.
If you look, my book's under Business, then Marketing & Entrepreneurship, all these
different subcategories. At almost any time, I'm number one in one category or
another. For you to be able to hang that label on yourself as an Amazon bestseller,
Amazon number one bestseller, generally people…as long as they hit it in some
subcategory…then they're claiming they're an Amazon bestseller. I will tell you that in
most places it seems like calling yourself an Amazon bestseller carries a little less
weight than it used to, but still, it's nice to have that.
For the New York Times, it's about sales within a single week. Their week, I think,
starts on Sunday and runs through Saturday, I'm pretty sure, is the way it goes.
I'm going to walk you through two campaigns. First, this pre-order campaign I
did, and then I'm going to walk you through an Amazon campaign. I did both. I
did them back-to-back. Let's get right to it.
This is what my pre-order page looked like. I borrowed this design from Brendon
Burchard who not only gave me permission but encouraged me to actually use this
design. We took Brendon's design, and we tweaked it and tested it. We ran
thousands and thousands of people through this, so we were able to do a lot of tests.
For instance, when we started off, this red text here was not red. We changed that to
red, and that was a winner. This text here had been blue, and we tested it against
being black, and that really worked. We tested the button text here, ended up with
'Get free book now'.
The way this came in, you come in, this video auto plays. I'm not going to play the
video for you right now, but the video auto-plays, and it basically says, "Hey, I'm
awesome. This book is awesome. My students have done all these amazing things,
and I've got this book, and I want to give it to you for free." The page worked really,
really well.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 7 --

TRANSCRIPT / Bonus / Book Launch / Bestseller Campaigns
One of the things…as you come in here, you'll see that we're rotating through
basically testimonials and blurbs for the book. As you sit on this page, these will rotate
through. As people are watching this video, because they'll come in here…I think this
video is, if my memory's right, about seven or eight minutes, something like that…
while they're watching it, they're also seeing us scroll through these testimonials.
Basically, this is what the offer comes down to is “You get the book for free, for
shipping and handling. This is the exact formula my students have used to generate
more than $500 million in sales. The weapons of mass influence. How to use the Seed
Launch. Just enter it, and you'll get this, plus my free bonuses.” I don't even talk about
really talk about the extra bonuses on here, but this page did really, really well.
Depending on traffic, we were converting about 36 to 40% of the people that hit this
page would actually go ahead and put their name and email address and then click
this button.
One of the things is…I'm giving away the book for free, but I'm charging shipping
and handling and I disclosed that in the video, very up front about it in the video, and
I'm also putting it right here. I will tell you, we did get people that came here, that
filled this out and then said, "What's the deal? Now you're asking me for money," but
if someone doesn't watch the video and doesn't read the text…there are those
people out there! You can't please everyone all the time.
In any case, this is the page. After they filled out their name and email address, they
came through here. This is basically just to ask for their name, first name, last name,
their email, their phone, their billing address, and then credit card information. We tell
them what they're getting, over here. We also tell them here's some of the bonuses.
We probably could've done a better job of spelling out that these were bonuses, that
they get the Case Study, and they get extra in-depth training videos that'll walk you
through Product Launch Formula. We give them a couple more testimonials, here.
That's it. Basically, they fill out their information, they click here.
The way the numbers worked out here is that, like I said, we had about 35 to 40% of
the people here gave us name and email address. Then we have people coming in
here, and the total of the people that actually hit this page to actually giving us credit
cards and getting the book, was 26%. 26% of the traffic that landed on this page gave
us a credit card. About 40% came through here, and then we had probably about
65% of the people that hit this page actually filled it out and gave us a credit card and
went through with the process. This is really, really powerful.
The next step, after they hit this, then they got this page, which says, "Thank you.
Please watch this video for the next step. It's just a few minutes long." Then what this
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 7 --

TRANSCRIPT / Bonus / Book Launch / Bestseller Campaigns
does is this makes an offer. I'm not going to play the whole video for you, but it's an
offer for $197, and it was a one-click special, where they came in, here. This button
was delayed. This is a short video. I forget exactly how long it was. I think it was
another five or six minutes. It tells them that they're going to get the book, and then it
makes an offer.
We did test different offers here. I can't tell you exactly what the offer is here, because
we tested different offers. This is Launching Your List 2.0. We sold them Launching
Your List 2.0 for $197, and they could either click here, and it was a one-click where
they click there and then they made the purchase, or they could say, "I'm not
interested in the offer." Then if they did that, they would go to the thank you page.
One of the key things here is this: Because we're going negative on the book here,
since we're giving the book away…well we're charging $6.95 for shipping and
handling, but the reality is, we had a lot of money we're spending in shipping and
handling and getting the books, so we were going negative, but once people hit
here, then we were able to more than break even, depending on the traffic. Some
traffic converted a lot higher than others. We had some lists where the traffic was
converting around 4%. We had other lists where the traffic was converting more like
12%.
If it's converting at 10% and a $200 offer, it's $197. If it was converting at 10%, to
where 10% of the people took this offer, that meant on average for each person
taking the offer, we were bringing in almost $20 from this upsell, and we were able to
give commission to our partners. This is a way we were able to get our partners to
mail for us, and we were able to give a commission here.
The other key is that a few weeks after this, after they've gone through this, then we
ran them through the sequence for Product Launch Formula, and that brought in
additional revenue. All told, we were able to bring in, I think it was about a million-
and-a-half dollars in revenue from giving these books away for free…ostensibly for
free…basically free plus shipping and handling.
There's a lot of different moving parts. I know I just threw a lot of numbers at you, but
just conceptually, it went from get the book for free with shipping and handling,
getting their credit card, giving them a one-click offer, something that they might be
interested in after they get the book. After that, then walking them through your
sequence for your full product. In this case, it was Product Launch Formula.
All-in-all, even though we gave away a lot of books and spent a lot of money on this,
we were able to more than break even. Now I will tell you, when you do this, you're
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 7 --

TRANSCRIPT / Bonus / Book Launch / Bestseller Campaigns
actually selling the books through your site. When you do this, if you want those
books to count for a bestseller list, then you have to use a service like Result
Source. They specialize in basically making sure your sales, when you sell them off
your site, that those sales actually count for the bestseller list.
Result Sources, they're real expensive to use because you have to buy the books.
You're buying the books at retail and shipping them out, so it's a very expensive
process, so we only use this for a very short period of time, and then we switched over
to the next campaign, which is the Amazon campaign, which I'm going to show you in
just a moment.
Again, there's lots and lots of moving parts. You have to have your math down really,
really well to do something like this. When I say math, I mean the idea that you
have an idea that you're going to be able to put together an offer that's going to
convert, with an upsell that's going to convert, with a backend offer that's going
to convert. That since you're spending money ahead of time…often you're spending
money ahead of time (like with this Launching Your List bonus, this upsell bonus, I was
bringing in money immediately, but still, you're spending a lot of money, a lot of
money flying around), so you really have to have your ducks in a row to do something
like this.
This is just the thank you page. There's four pages here. There's this very first page
to get the free book with the video, then this is where you collect the credit card,
then you have a one-click upsell, then this is just a thank you page telling them
what to expect, that you're going to be getting the book as soon as I get them from a
publisher. I'll be sending your bonuses via email, and just a standard thank you page
telling them what to expect.
Then what we did is we quickly switched over to the Amazon campaign. This Amazon
campaign, and initially it didn't have this yellow border up here. We added this after
we hit the bestseller list, so we initially did not have that. This page looks a lot like that
first page. Again, another video here, but in this case what we're doing is we're
driving traffic to this page, and we're asking our partners to drive traffic to this page.
Then this video basically sells them on the idea of the book. Then if they click here,
they'll go over to Amazon and then once they buy it on Amazon, it gives them
instructions on how to get bonuses.
Basically the offer here is go buy it on Amazon, but then send me an email and
I'm going to send you some extra bonuses. This is a very bonus-driven
promotion. The only problem here is you're relying on people, leaving your site,
going to Amazon, buying the book on Amazon, then coming back to your site and
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 7 --

TRANSCRIPT / Bonus / Book Launch / Bestseller Campaigns
opting in to your site. Then once they did that, then I would give them bonuses, and
then I would walk them through the sequence for Product Launch Formula and
monetize it that way.
Again, in some ways, this is sort of like the...the backend portion of this [initial
promotion], where after they bought, then they went through the PLF sequence. Here
[Amazon promotion], they go buy the book, get the book on Amazon, they email in,
then we send them bonuses. First, we give them a Case Study, and then we send
them the pre-launch, just the Evergreen Pre-launch Sequence for Product Launch
Formula. That is the Amazon campaign.
A lot fewer moving parts to this one, a lot simpler. You're not spending a lot of money
up front. There's just fewer moving parts in this one. In this case, the way you do it is
you're mailing your list, and then you're asking your partners to mail their lists, and
you're just trying to drive all your traffic within a very short period of time to drive
yourself up to the top of Amazon.
